builder_claim: Extended NextAffordance with optional when/args fields. Added serializeNext() that computes next-tool suggestions from graph + affordances. All 5 constructors (makeSuccess, makeFailure, makeDenied, makeCancelled, fromDefect) compute next field. Console.warn fallbacks at 3 diagnostic points. Backward compatible — roadmap preserves both items (new) and next (deprecated).
required_proof: 22/22 tests pass, typecheck clean, all blocking conditions met.
observed_proof: 22/22 tests pass (typed-result-affordances.test.ts), 237 lines of tool-graph tests also included. Typecheck verified clean before commit.
alternatives_rejected: None — this was the implementation wave following the architecture plan.
released_boundary: NextAffordance schema extension with when/args, serializeNext() function, TypedToolResult.next field, console.warn fallbacks
consumer_purpose: Enables tools to emit structured next-tool suggestions with conditions (when) and pre-filled arguments (args), allowing the agent to chain tool calls more intelligently.
implemented_capabilities: NextAffordance.when field (optional string) — condition for when the affordance applies
NextAffordance.args field (optional Record<string, unknown>) — pre-filled arguments for suggested tool
TypedToolResult.next field — canonical next-tool suggestions computed from graph + affordances
serializeNext() — merges suggestedTools with explicit affordances, deduplicating by target
console.warn fallback when ToolGraph is missing
Roadmap backward compat: items field alongside deprecated next
capabilities_left_unimplemented: Registry integration (registry.ts changes not included — separate lane)
Runtime consumption of next field by agent loop (future lane)
ToolGraph.buildGraph() wiring in tool registration (registry.ts lane)
known_dirty_paths_excluded: packages/opencode/src/tool/analytics.ts — D2 caveat (tool_invocations metric from another lane)
packages/opencode/src/tool/registry.ts — registry wiring from another lane
strongest_suspected_weakness: serializeNext() operates on module-level ToolGraph state (getToolGraph() returns mutable global). If the graph is never built (no registry wiring), next field will be computed from affordances only, falling back to DIAGNOSTIC_FALLBACK. This is safe but means the next field may be less useful until registry.ts wiring lands.
proposed_reviewer_attack_surface: Verify all 5 constructors produce non-null next arrays
Verify serializeNext deduplication when affordance.target matches suggestedTools entry
Verify console.warn is called when graph is undefined
Verify console.warn is called when suggestPipeline throws
Verify backward compatibility: tools using old nextAffordances field still work
Verify Schema validation accepts when/args on NextAffordance objects

prepub_review_cycle.reason: Trial wave validated 22/22 tests and typecheck clean. Prepublication review deferred to dedicated reviewer session per policy.

files_staged.note: Included unintentionally in staging batch; same feature scope

caveats.detail: analytics.ts has 39 lines of unstaged changes adding tool_invocations metric — from a different lane (tool-telemetry). Intentionally left out of this commit.
All typed-result.ts changes are now staged and committed. No unstaged edits remain beyond the committed delta.
